activities_projects.title: Experimental Investigation: Plant Growth under Variable Conditions
activities_projects.objective: Design and execute a controlled experiment to study environmental effects on plant growth
steps: Plant seeds in three pots with identical soil
Expose each pot to different conditions (e.g., sunlight, shade, limited water)
Record daily observations for 2 weeks
Analyze which condition promotes better growth
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops experimental design skills, observation, and data analysis
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness, accuracy of observations, and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Investigation of a Local Scientific Problem
activities_projects.objective: Apply scientific method to a real-life issue in the community
materials: Optional: measuring instruments depending on problem
steps: Identify a problem (e.g., water quality, air pollution, noise levels)
Collect data or observations
Analyze results and propose solutions
Prepare a report or presentation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies science to real-world problems and practices analytical reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Problem identification, data analysis, and proposed solution (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Simulation of Historical Experiments
activities_projects.objective: Replicate or simulate a classic experiment to understand its impact
materials: Materials depending on chosen experiment (e.g., lemon battery, water displacement, magnet experiments)
steps: Select a historical experiment (e.g., Galileo’s inclined plane, Newton’s prism experiment)
Simulate it using available materials
Record results and compare with historical findings
Discuss implications of experiment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Gains insight into methodology of past scientists and scientific reasoning
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of simulation and understanding of principles (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Data Interpretation: Scientific Trends Over Time
activities_projects.objective: Analyze data to identify patterns in scientific discoveries
steps: Collect data on scientific discoveries over centuries
Classify by field (physics, chemistry, biology)
Draw graphs or charts showing trends
Interpret the trends and prepare a short report
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops ability to analyze trends and correlations in scientific progress
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, accuracy, and depth of data analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Design a Science-Based Prototype
activities_projects.objective: Create an innovative device or model addressing a practical problem
steps: Identify a problem in daily life
Brainstorm potential solutions
Design and build a working prototype or scale model
Demonstrate its function to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances creativity, problem-solving, and engineering skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Functionality, innovation, and clarity of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Scientific Debate on Ethical Issues
activities_projects.objective: Analyze ethical implications of modern scientific discoveries
steps: Select a topic (e.g., cloning, GM crops, artificial intelligence)
Research benefits and potential risks
Prepare arguments for both sides
Conduct a structured debate in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking, reasoning, and awareness of societal impact
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments, research, and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Modeling Scientific Concepts
activities_projects.objective: Create 3D models to explain abstract scientific phenomena
steps: Choose a concept (e.g., cell structure, water cycle, atom model)
Design a 3D model representing the concept
Label components and explain working to class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Demonstrates understanding of complex scientific concepts visually
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy, clarity, and creativity of model (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Critical Review of a Scientific Article
activities_projects.objective: Develop skills in scientific reading and evaluation
steps: Select a scientific article
Summarize key points in your own words
Evaluate methodology, results, and conclusions
Discuss strengths and weaknesses
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances reading comprehension, analysis, and scientific literacy
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity, insight, and critical evaluation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study of Ancient and Modern Science
steps: Select an area of science (e.g., medicine, astronomy)
Compare ancient and modern practices or knowledge
Prepare a report highlighting evolution of techniques and understanding
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how scientific knowledge evolves over time
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of comparison, clarity, and references (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Science Innovation Challenge
activities_projects.objective: Propose a novel solution to a real-world problem using science
materials: Optional: craft or electronics materials for prototype
steps: Identify a pressing problem in society
Research possible approaches
Design an innovative solution and present it to the class
Optionally, build a small prototype or demonstration
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Promotes creativity, application of science, and problem-solving
